Captcha systems are designed to prevent spam by requiring users to prove that they are human. This is usually done by showing the user an image with distorted text or a series of images with a specific pattern and asking them to enter the text or select the images that match the pattern. 
Spammers might try to use automated programs to send out large numbers of spam messages, but these programs would not be able to solve the captcha. Therefore, captcha systems can effectively block spam because they can distinguish between human users and automated programs. 
One reason that captcha systems are difficult to break is that they can be designed to be very difficult for a computer to interpret. For example, the text in the image might be distorted in a way that is easy for a human to read but hard for a computer to recognize. Similarly, the pattern of images might be difficult for a computer to recognize without the context that a human would naturally bring to the task. 
Overall, captcha systems are an effective way to prevent spam because they require humans to prove that they are human, which is something that automated programs cannot do.

Steam is a digital distribution platform for video games and other forms of entertainment. It is owned by Valve Corporation, a company based in the United States. Steam has a user-based market where people can buy and sell in-game items and other virtual goods using a virtual currency called "Steam dollars." When a user buys something on the Steam market, they pay for it using Steam dollars, which are stored in their Steam wallet. Steam charges a transaction fee for each purchase made on the market. This fee is a percentage of the total purchase price, and it is used to help cover the cost of running the market and maintaining the Steam platform. To turn a profit, Valve Corporation needs to convert the Steam dollars that it has earned from transaction fees into real legal currency, such as US dollars. They can do this by exchanging the Steam dollars for real money through a financial institution. This process is known as "cashing out." So, in summary, Steam earns profit by charging a transaction fee for purchases made on the Steam market. They then convert this virtual currency into real money through the process of cashing out.

Wind turbines use the energy of the wind to generate electricity. The wind pushes against the blades of the turbine, causing them to spin. The spinning motion is transferred to a generator inside the turbine, which converts the mechanical energy of the spinning blades into electrical energy. The speed at which the blades of a wind turbine spin is not constant. It depends on the strength and direction of the wind. However, most wind turbines have a mechanism that helps them spin at a consistent speed, even if the wind speed changes. This is important because generators work most efficiently when they are spinning at a consistent speed. The mechanism that helps wind turbines spin at a consistent speed is called a "pitch control system." The pitch control system adjusts the angle of the blades as they spin, so that they can capture the most energy from the wind. If the wind is strong, the pitch control system will adjust the blades to a more streamlined position, so that they can spin faster without being damaged. If the wind is weaker, the pitch control system will adjust the blades to a more upright position, so that they can capture more of the wind's energy. So, to answer your question, wind turbines do not always spin at a constant rate. However, they are designed to spin at a consistent speed, even if the wind changes, by using a pitch control system to adjust the angle of the blades.

DSL (Digital Subscriber Line) technology is a type of broadband internet connection that uses regular telephone wires to transmit data. While regular telephone lines are not typically designed for high-speed internet, DSL technology is able to achieve relatively fast speeds by using advanced digital signal processing techniques.To understand how this works, it's helpful to first understand how regular telephone lines work. Telephone lines are made up of two wires, one for transmitting signals and one for receiving signals. When you speak into a telephone, your voice is converted into an electrical signal, which is sent through the transmitting wire to the person on the other end of the line. When the person on the other end speaks, their voice is converted into an electrical signal and sent through the receiving wire back to you.DSL technology is able to use these same telephone lines to transmit data by sending digital signals over the wires instead of analog signals (like the sound of your voice). The digital signals can be transmitted at much higher frequencies than the analog signals used for phone calls, which allows for faster data transfer speeds.DSL technology is able to achieve high speeds by using advanced techniques to filter out interference and noise from other signals that might be present on the same telephone line. This allows the DSL signals to be transmitted more clearly and at higher speeds, even over regular telephone wires.I hope this helps! Do you have any other questions about this?

Valuation is slightly different to ' worth ' . Valuation is determined purely by the funding round . When someone buys a stake in the business the valuation is calculated by how much they paid for a specific percentage of the business . Regardless of the previous valuation of Uber , if someone invests $ 1 billion for 1 % of the business , the valuation is now $ 100 billion ( or 100 x the $ 1 billion investment ) . Effectively , their investment is the only thing that 's increased the valuation . Had they bought the same percentage for twice as much money , the new valuation would be $ 200 billion . With Uber , whoever bought into the company at a $ 62 billion valuation is confident the business will grow in value and the value of their stake will also increase . Whether Uber is worth a valuation of $ 62 billion is a different matter . They are growing every year ( I think 200 % revenue growth last year ) , which is a good sign . And there is potential for much more growth as they move into new countries and cities , which is also a good sign . They are also hedging their bets by investing into driverless technology , which will potentially safeguard them when the marketplace adapts to that new technology , which is a wise move . As to the true worth of the business , that remains to be seen . All we know so far is what a small number of investors think they are worth . They could go for an IPO this year and that 's when we 'll see what the market thinks they are worth .

There isn't a single reason why women generally have longer hair than men. Different cultures have different traditions and expectations when it comes to how men and women wear their hair.

In some cultures, long hair is associated with femininity and is seen as a sign of beauty for women. This may be because long hair is thought to be softer and more attractive than short hair. In other cultures, long hair may be seen as a sign of strength, power, or spiritual devotion.

In contrast, in some cultures, short hair is associated with masculinity and is seen as a sign of strength and power. This may be because short hair is thought to be easier to maintain and more practical for certain activities, such as sports or manual labor.

It's important to remember that these cultural associations with long and short hair are not universal and can change over time. What is considered attractive or appropriate for men and women to wear their hair can vary greatly depending on the culture and time period.

In a few words , the belief that everything is inherently meaningless , and any semblance of significance is entirely subjective and derived by the individual experiencing it . Example : We 're alone in the universe ( which will some day fold in on itself and invalidate the entirety of human achievement ) , we 're going to die someday ( before immortality is discovered , and even the greatest among us will be little more than a name mostly uttered out of context ) and none of us can ever truly be sure someone feels the way we do or loves us like we love them . But right now , I 'm watching my cat do stupid shit and it 's making me laugh , so I 've got that going for me , which is nice ... * * TL;DR :* * Life is entirely what you make it . * * EDIT :* * Realized I may have lost my initial point in my meandering prose . What I was getting at is , despite all evidence that life may have no actual meaning , existentialism dictates you can find that meaning anywhere , simply by appreciating the experiences existence has to offer ( hence , the name ) . So , life is basically meaningless . But thanks to my cat 's silly antics , I had to pause , reflect , shamelessly lift a Kurt Vonnegut quote and think " If this is n't nice , I do n't know what is . " And in finding joy in a mundane moment , I 've manufactured my own meaning from out of the darkness .

Lights can make a humming noise for a variety of reasons. Here are a few possibilities:

The light bulbs themselves may be the cause of the humming. If the bulbs are loose in their sockets or are not compatible with the fixtures, they may vibrate and cause a humming noise.

The electrical wiring in the room may be the cause of the humming. If the wiring is damaged or improperly installed, it can cause a humming noise.

The light fixtures themselves may be the cause of the humming. If the fixtures are not securely mounted or are damaged, they may vibrate and cause a humming noise.

The source of the electricity that powers the lights may be the cause of the humming. If the electrical source is experiencing problems, it can cause a humming noise.

It is important to note that humming lights can sometimes be a sign of a more serious issue, such as a problem with the electrical system in your home. If the humming noise is persistent or becomes louder over time, it is a good idea to have it checked out by a professional electrician to ensure that everything is working safely and properly.

Timeshares are not necessarily a scam, but they can be difficult for some people to use or sell, and they may not always provide the value that was promised. Here's how timeshares work: 1. You buy a timeshare, which gives you the right to use a vacation property (like a condo or a house) for a certain number of weeks each year. 2. You pay a one-time purchase price and then annual fees to maintain the property and access to it. 3. You can usually choose to use your timeshare at the same property every year or exchange it for a different property through a network of affiliated resorts. Some people enjoy timeshares because they can plan their vacations in advance and have a comfortable place to stay. However, some people may find that they are unable to use their timeshare as much as they thought they would, or that the maintenance fees and other costs are too expensive. Additionally, it can be difficult to sell a timeshare, and some people may end up paying more for their timeshare than they get back when they try to sell it. Overall, it's important to carefully consider the costs and benefits of a timeshare before making a purchase, and to do your research to make sure you are making an informed decision.

Sure! There are many different types of wine, and they can be paired with different types of food to enhance the flavors of both. Here's a quick overview of some common types of wine and some general guidelines for pairing them with food: White wine: White wines are made from white grapes and tend to have a light, crisp flavor. They can be paired with lighter foods like seafood, chicken, and salads. Some examples of white wines include chardonnay, sauvignon blanc, and pinot grigio. Red wine: Red wines are made from red grapes and tend to have a bolder, fuller flavor. They can be paired with heartier foods like red meats, pasta dishes, and cheeses. Some examples of red wines include cabernet sauvignon, merlot, and pinot noir. Rosé wine: Rosé wines are made from red grapes but are not fermented for as long as red wines, so they have a pinkish color and a lighter flavor. They can be paired with a variety of foods, including seafood, salads, and grilled vegetables. Sparkling wine: Sparkling wines are bubbly and can be white or pink in color. They are often served as a celebratory drink or with appetizers and desserts. Examples of sparkling wines include champagne and prosecco. In general, it's a good idea to match the weight and intensity of the wine with the weight and intensity of the food. So, for example, a light white wine might be a good match for a light seafood dish, while a bold red wine might be a good match for a hearty steak. Hope this helps!

Planets stay in their orbital path because of the force of gravity. Imagine that you are standing on a trampoline and you toss a basketball up into the air. The basketball will go up, then it will come back down because the trampoline is pulling it towards the ground. In the same way, the planet is constantly being pulled towards the sun, but it never actually gets to the sun because it is moving too fast. It's like the basketball on the trampoline; it goes up and then it comes back down, but it never actually touches the ground.

The reason the planet stays in its orbit is because it has a certain amount of energy and momentum, which is like a type of force that keeps it moving in a circle around the sun. As long as the planet has enough energy and momentum, it will stay in its orbit and not fall into the sun.

Satellites, which are objects that orbit around a planet or other celestial body, work in a similar way. They stay in their orbital path because they have energy and momentum, just like planets. However, satellites can sometimes fall out of their orbit if they lose too much energy or momentum, or if they are affected by other forces such as friction or gravity from other objects.

Some people also think that DC should be given more control over its own affairs, like the ability to pass its own laws and have its own representatives in Congress. Graduate student in computer science here . Ignore what anyone tells you about nondeterministic polynomial time , big O notation , etc . That 's like trying to explain a chemical reaction to a layman who does n't know what hydrogen is . The best explanation is this : consider something like writing music . It is very hard to write good music , but most humans can verify with just a few seconds of listening ( genre preferences aside ) that a piece is indeed " good music . " The same applies to cooking - given a batch of ingredients , it is very hard to cook something that tastes good , but takes just one bite to confirm it . The P = NP problem is the equivalent for computer science problems . Is finding something ( a solution in this instance ) as difficult as verifying ? Why is this interesting to humanity ? Well a lot of problems can be shown to be of this form . That is , we know that it is easy to verify a solution , but coming up with the solution seems " difficult . " For example certain instances of scheduling delivery routes , organ donor / exchange problems , etc . can all be shown to be of this form . Even more interesting is that all these problems are related . If we can solve one of them " easily , " we can solve all the others " easily . " The last example I give is my favorite . Someone gives you 10 Tetris pieces , and asks you to make a perfect " Tetris , " that is a 4 high by 10 wide block with them . This may not be easy and might take you a while . But if someone showed you the solution , it would take a millisecond for you to realize " Oh ! It is doable , and that 's how it 's done . "

The rods and cones in your eye are light-sensitive cells that help you see. They are located in a layer at the back of your eye called the retina. When light enters your eye, it passes through the cornea (the clear front part of your eye) and the pupil (the black circle in the center of your eye). The light then hits the retina, which sends a signal to your brain through the optic nerve. Your brain processes the signal and interprets it as an image. The rods and cones in your eye are responsible for detecting different wavelengths of light and sending this information to your brain. The rods are more sensitive to low levels of light and are responsible for helping you see in low light conditions, while the cones are responsible for helping you see color and fine detail. The reason that the image in the URL you provided appears to move and change when you look at it is because of an optical illusion called the "photon wave function collapse." This illusion is created by the interaction of the light-sensitive cells in your eye with the patterns and colors in the image. When you look at the image, your brain is trying to interpret the patterns and colors, but it is unable to do so because the patterns and colors are constantly changing. This causes your brain to perceive the image as moving and changing, even though it is actually still.

An ISP, or Internet Service Provider, is a company that provides access to the internet. Think of it like a pipeline that connects your computer or device to the internet.

To connect to the internet, you need a way to send and receive data with other computers and servers around the world. An ISP provides that connection by setting up special servers and networks that can communicate with the rest of the internet.

It's possible to be your own ISP, but it's not easy. You would need to set up your own servers and networks, and make sure they are connected to the rest of the internet. This requires a lot of technical knowledge and resources, so most people and businesses choose to use an ISP instead.

Another reason is that the orcs are often fighting against more powerful and skilled opponents. But if you taste a spoonful of pure vanilla extract by itself, it can taste a bit too strong and overpowering. That's because it's not meant to be eaten on its own, but rather used as a way to add flavor to other foods.

As for some cool vanilla recipes, here are a few ideas:

Vanilla Bean Fudge: Melt some chocolate and mix in a spoonful of vanilla extract to make delicious vanilla fudge.

Vanilla Smoothie: Blend together your favorite fruit, a splash of milk, a spoonful of vanilla extract, and some ice to make a tasty vanilla smoothie.

Vanilla Sugar Cookies: Mix together some flour, sugar, butter, an egg, and a splash of vanilla extract to make soft and sweet vanilla sugar cookies.

Vanilla Ice Cream: Mix together some heavy cream, milk, sugar, and vanilla extract, then freeze it in an ice cream maker for a creamy and delicious vanilla ice cream.

Analog is just a signal that can have any value , ideally between two given points . To make it digital , you need to sample that . You have a bit depth , which the more bits the more different levels of the analog signal you can record . You have a sample rate , which is how many samples you have in a given time , usually by a second . For example , a compact disc is 16 bits , which means each channel has 65,536 distinct audio levels , in the neighborhood of a 3V spread . It has a sample rate of 44.1 Khz , meaning there are 44,100 samples each second .

When you smoke cigarettes, you are inhaling a lot of harmful chemicals and substances into your body. These substances can irritate and damage your respiratory system, including your lungs and throat.

When you quit smoking, your body starts to repair itself and get rid of these harmful substances. This process can sometimes cause symptoms such as coughing, sneezing, and a runny nose. These symptoms may be unpleasant, but they are actually a good sign that your body is getting healthier and starting to heal.

Think of it like this: when you stop smoking, your body is like a house that has been dirty and cluttered for a long time. It takes a little bit of time and effort to clean up the mess and get everything in order, but once it's done, the house (or in this case, your body) is a much healthier and happier place to be.

Daylight saving time is a way to make better use of the daylight hours in the summer. During the summer, the days are longer, which means that the sun rises and sets later. This means that the hours when it is light outside are longer, but the hours when it is dark are shorter.

Daylight saving time is a way to shift the clock so that the hours when it is light outside are even longer. When we "spring forward" and set the clocks ahead by one hour, it stays light outside for an extra hour in the evenings. This can be helpful because it gives people more time to do outdoor activities or just enjoy the sunshine.

In the fall, when we "fall back" and set the clocks back by one hour, it gets dark outside earlier. This can be helpful because it means that people are more likely to be awake and inside when it is dark outside, which can save energy because they are not using lights as much.

Overall, the purpose of daylight saving time is to make better use of the daylight hours and to save energy.

There are many potential dangers associated with artificial intelligence (AI), and it is important to consider these risks as we develop and use AI technology. 
One potential danger is that AI could be used to harm people. For example, if an AI system is used to control a military drone or a self-driving car, it could make decisions that result in injury or death. 
Another potential danger is that AI could be used to spread misinformation or manipulate people. For example, an AI system could create fake news stories or social media posts that are designed to influence people's opinions or behavior. 
A third potential danger is that AI could lead to the loss of jobs. As AI systems become more advanced, they may be able to perform tasks that are currently done by humans. This could result in many people losing their jobs and potentially struggling to find new employment. 
A fourth potential danger is that AI could be used to violate people's privacy. For example, an AI system could be used to gather personal information about people without their knowledge or consent, and this information could be used for malicious purposes. 
Overall, it is important to carefully consider the potential risks and dangers associated with AI, and to take steps to mitigate these risks as we develop and use AI technology.

" Executive privilege " refers to the notion that the President of the United States ( and by extension certain other members of the Executive Branch ) does n't necessarily have to respond to subpoenas issued by the courts or Congress . There are two basic kinds of executive privilege : deliberative and communications . " Deliberative privilege " or " deliberative process privilege " protects the President , and potentially * any * executive - branch decision - maker from having to divulge information about the process by which certain important decisions are made . The idea is that in order to make fully - informed decisions , the the President and executive agencies need to be able to fully hash things out . Brainstorming , basically . Brainstorming invariably involves people floating ideas that turn out to be * bad * ideas . Sometimes it involves suggesting courses of action or relying upon sources of information that , in hindsight , are improper . But that 's an essential part of the process in identifying * good * decisions , so the US legal system exempts certain materials related to " deliberative processes " from disclosure . " Communications privilege " protects the President from having to divulge communications that he himself conducts with his immediate advisors : cabinet - level officials , immediate aides , White House Counsel , etc . This is viewed as being inherent in the separation of powers which is a fundamental feature of the form of government created by the US Constitution . If Congress or the Courts could force the President or his immediate advisors to disclose all of their communications , it would be practically impossible for the President to do his job . This is privilege is much , much harder to overcome than deliberative process privilege .

A slightly different answer : Species evolve qualities that are beneficial for survival , and passing on their genes . That 's the only reason a particular trait gets selected and passed on in a species . Humans developed large front brains ( which make us intelligent ) , because intelligence confers advantages for surviving and passing on our genes . So , humans developed big , smart brains for the same reason that we developed arms , that rhinos developed thick skin , that alligators developed an incredibly slow metabolism , and that owls developed acute vision . All of these traits are very helpful for helping the creature survive and mate . So , our asking " Why are n't other species as intelligent as us ? " is very much like an alligator asking " Why do n't other creatures have as slow metabolisms as we do ? " or an owl asking " Why ca n't other creatures see or fly as well as we do ? " . The answer is that those traits are n't the ones that were helpful for those creatures to survive . Each creature developed the traits it did in order to overcome environmental challenges to survival , and it happens that intelligence was a huge help for primates in trying to survive , but not so much for alligators . Alligators do n't need to be able to make tools or to fly to weather their environmental challenges . What would be more helpful is if they could slow their metabolism to the point that they only need to eat about once a year - and this is the trait they developed . The same goes for us . Intelligence is one of many evolutionary tools developed for survival , but by no means the most efficient one . Bacteria - stupid as they are - are better at surviving than we are .

We know that the universe is expanding and a certain rate and we can tell which direction it is expanded towards . Understanding how we can prove this is probably futile unless you have a degree in astrophysics . However the concept is similar to tracing the origin of any projectile . If you know someone threw a ball at 90 mph and the direction in which it was thrown you can trace back it 's origin . We do the same with the universe . So we now we know the origin of the universe , along with the speed of the universe and the ' end ' of the universe we can easily calculate a time that it 's been moving and thus we get an age . Some other notes that are just some decent background : How do you get something from nothing ? Well we do n't really , we know that every time an electron is created it 's antimatter partner ( positron ) is also created . That 's like changing the equation 0=0 to -1 +1 = 0 . Nothing really changed . HOWEVER we know that 1 in 100 million times or so we created an electron without a positron . We are still looking into why this is . The Big Bang also fits into many of the formulas and theories we have created to explain the universe . So that 's good evidence that it happened . Finally the way science works is by testing the hypothesis and trying to prove it to not be possible . Can we prove that a tree makes a sound when no one is around ? Well I suppose not , but we can create formulas for the generation of a sound wave and learn that sound is due to air molecules vibrating . From there we try to make a tree fall without vibrating molecules and essentially find we ca n't do it unless we 're in a vacuum ( but we already know sound ca n't pass through without a medium ) . So from there we have to go with the assumption that all falling trees make a sound .

No , photons are created and destroyed all the time . Photons are " destroyed " when they are absorbed by another particle , typically an electron in an atom . Electrons have different orbits they can occupy based on their energy , and when they absorb an electron , it kicks them up to a higher energy orbit . URL_1 They are created in the opposite process , when a higher energy particle ( again , most often an electron ) falls to a lower energy state . The energy it loses is given off as a photon . This can happen spontaneously , or when stimulated by another similar photon that happens to be passing by ( as in a laser ) URL_0 URL_2 As for where the photons " go " when you turn off the light , they 're absorbed by the materials that they hit . When a photon hits a surface , some fraction are reflected ( that 's how you see things , the photons are reflected off them toward your eyes ) , some fraction are absorbed , and some fraction can be transmitted through the material . So in a room , the light is creating photons , and those photons strike the materials in the room and either get reflected towards another surface , or get absorbed ( or transmitted if you have a window or something ) . Eventually the photons get absorbed by something . When you turn off the light , since there are n't any more photons being generated , when the existing photons get absorbed , the room goes dark .

They fought against pro - Britain groups , primarily the Ulster Volunteer Force ( UVF ) , who used similar guerrilla tactics . This system uses sensors and computers to determine the rover's or probe's position and orientation in space, and to adjust its trajectory as needed to stay on course.

Another important technology is a landing system, which helps the rover or probe to land safely on the asteroid's surface. This might include a parachute to slow the descent, or thrusters to control the speed and direction of the landing.

To land on an asteroid, the rover or probe first needs to be launched into space and travel to the asteroid. Once it gets close to the asteroid, it uses its guidance and navigation system to find the right spot to land, and then uses its landing system to touch down gently on the asteroid's surface.

Even with these technologies and techniques, landing a rover or probe on an asteroid is still a difficult and risky endeavor. But by carefully planning and executing the mission, NASA and other space agencies are able to successfully land rovers and probes on asteroids, and gather valuable scientific data and samples from these fascinating and mysterious objects.

In communism , everyone works as hard as they can or want and then shares the production . It 's great in that no one gets screwed out of their share because they were n't born with the right talents or whatever , but it 's bad in that if the big / smart / strong guy decides to be lazy , he still gets his share , so he 's not as motivated to work hard as he would be if he only got what he produced . Marxism is a particular kind of communism . This guy Karl Marx wrote a book about communism and how it should come about . It starts with a revolution , then a strong central government gets people used to the idea of not working for themselves , before eventually transitioning to communism . No " communist " country has ever totally made that transition . Fascism is different , because it 's not about production and money and things , it 's more a political philosophy . Fascists think a country works best when you have a strong national identity and everyone works together because they 're all the same . But to get everyone thinking they 're all the same , you kind of have to suppress differences . Class , race , language , all sorts of things , you have to either make it so people are all the same , or make it seem they 're all the same . Individuality is the enemy of fascists .

We would n't have a concrete frame - rate . We have different components in our eyes , like rods and cones , that all update independently and at different rates . So in one instant , a thousand of your cones might be updating ; the next instant , a hundred cones and a hundred rods might be , and so on . Then we have our brain , which does n't process individual neat frames , but kind of blurs everything together as a constant stream , with the focus of our vision given higher priority than things in our peripheral vision . So not only does the ' frame rate ' differ moment to moment and cell to cell , it differs on different areas of the eye . Compare this to a computer or TV , which updates an exact 50/60/100/120 etc times per second ( depending on model ) every single second , the entire screen at once , and a computer graphics card , which builds one complete frame , sends it , builds the next complete frame , sends it ... It 's not a very fun answer , but we really just ca n't describe our eyes in terms of a framerate .

The speed of sound is a constant value because it depends on the properties of the medium through which the sound travels. The medium can be a gas, liquid, or solid, and the speed of sound is different for each type of medium. In a gas, like air, the speed of sound is determined by the temperature and pressure of the gas. When a sound wave travels through a gas, it is caused by the vibration of particles in the gas. The faster the particles are moving, the faster the sound wave will travel. The temperature and pressure of the gas can affect the speed of the particles, and therefore the speed of the sound wave. In a liquid or a solid, the speed of sound is determined by the density and stiffness of the material. When a sound wave travels through a liquid or solid, it is caused by the vibration of the particles in the material. The denser and stiffer the material is, the faster the sound wave will travel. The speed of sound is always constant within a given medium because the properties of the medium do not change quickly. If the temperature, pressure, density, or stiffness of the medium changes, it will take some time for the change to affect the speed of the sound wave. The difference between 32-bit and 64-bit refers to the way a computer's processor (also called a CPU), handles information. A 32-bit system can process data in chunks of 32 bits at a time, while a 64-bit system can process data in chunks of 64 bits at a time. This makes a 64-bit system faster and more efficient than a 32-bit system, especially when running tasks that require a lot of processing power. In terms of downloading a program, the difference between the 32-bit and 64-bit versions is that the 32-bit version can only be installed on a computer with a 32-bit processor, while the 64-bit version can only be installed on a computer with a 64-bit processor. If you try to install the wrong version (e.g., a 32-bit program on a 64-bit computer), the installation will fail. To summarize, the main difference between 32-bit and 64-bit systems is the amount of data that can be processed at once and the type of processor required to run the program. A 32-bit system can handle less data at once and requires a 32-bit processor, while a 64-bit system can handle more data at once and requires a 64-bit processor.

Cars only have defroster grids in the rear window because the front windshield is made of different material than the rear window. The front windshield is made of laminated safety glass, which is two layers of glass with a plastic layer in between. The laminated safety glass is designed to stay in one piece even if it cracks or breaks, so it doesn't need a defroster grid to help clear it. 
The rear window, on the other hand, is usually made of tempered glass, which is a type of glass that shatters into small pieces if it breaks. Tempered glass is used in the rear window because it is stronger and more durable than regular glass, but it is also more prone to fogging up or getting covered in condensation. That's why the rear window has a defroster grid, which is a grid of heating elements that help clear the window of fog or condensation. 
So, in short, cars have defroster grids in the rear window because it is made of a different type of glass that is more prone to fogging up, and because the front windshield is made of laminated safety glass that doesn't need a defroster grid.

They 'll take blurry photos for two reasons . One , they 're not properly focused . Two , because the camera moved while the shutter was open . Cameras need different shutter speeds because lighting conditions change , and there are two ways you can get more light into the camera -- make the aperture the light goes through bigger , or make the exposure time longer . However , if you ca n't open the aperture in the camera wide enough , then you have to increase the exposure time . And , just to make it more fun , the wider the aperture the less depth of field an image has . " Depth of field " refers to the size of the area , measured away from the front of the camera lens , that will all be in focus in one shot . With a very small aperture , you might get virtually infinite depth of field , but you also have to use a slower shutter speed and blur . With a large aperture and a fast shutter speed , you may still get an out - of - focus image because , for example , only an area between three and four feet in front of the camera will be in focus .

Water is considered the "universal solvent" because it can dissolve many different substances. This is because the molecules in water are polar, which means that one end of the molecule has a positive charge and the other end has a negative charge. Substances that dissolve in water are usually also polar, or have ionic bonds, which means that they have positive and negative charges as well. When these substances come into contact with water, the positive and negative charges on the water molecules and the substance attract each other, causing the substance to dissolve. For example, when you add salt to water, the positive and negative charges in the salt molecules are attracted to the opposite charges on the water molecules. The salt dissolves in the water and becomes evenly distributed throughout the liquid. Water is able to dissolve so many different substances because it is a polar molecule, and many substances are also polar or have ionic bonds. This makes it a very useful substance for mixing and dissolving other substances.

When Germanic tribes ( from present day northern Germany , Denmark , and the Netherlands ) , such as the Angels , the Saxons ( Hence " Anglo - Saxon " and " England " ) , and the Jutes conquered England , their language dominated and pushed the Celtic language to the west of the island , to what was to become Wales . This was " Old English , " and was very similar to other West Germanic languages at the time ( such as German , Dutch , and Frisian ) . In 1066 , after the Norman conquest , French was the language of the government , and the " prestige " language ( French was spoken by the royalty and nobility , and English by the peasants)--the Norman presence gave English thousands of words , distinguishing it from its other West Germanic counterparts . Even today , we can see influences of this prestige . For example , " begin " is of Germanic origin , while " commence " is French . The peasants did the work ( Germanic ) , while the French nobility took the profit . The peasants raised cows and pigs , and the French - speaking upper class ate the meats , beef and pork . TL;DR -- English is a mix of French and languages spoken by Germanic tribes that conquered post - Roman Britain .

Finally, the sensation of falling in dreams may be related to the way our brains process and interpret sensory information while we are asleep. During sleep, our brains are still receiving and processing sensory information from our surroundings, even though we are not consciously aware of it. This sensory information may be incorporated into our dreams and may contribute to the vividness and realism of the dream experience. 
Overall, the sensation of falling in dreams is a normal and common experience that is caused by a combination of factors related to body position, brain activity, and sensory processing during sleep.

Classes are generally divided into four broad categories : - The " Upper class " ; which does n't need to work for a living . Nobles in old times ; business owners , senior management , etc . in modern times . The lower levels of the Upper Class manage people , but they are n't " working " ; they 're " Managing " ( though since the 1960s ; there are " Middle managers " ; who are n't upper class ) . - The " Middle Class " ; people who work for a living based on investments in themselves : high - skilled labor , small business owners , middle managers ( who manage based on education , rather than based on connections ) , etc . Historically , this was the " Merchant class " ; occupied by wealthy merchants and guildmasters . - the " Working class " ; people who work for a living , often with their hands , but there 's a lot of working class jobs that are based on college degrees now . - The " Poor " : people who are n't making a living , either because they do n't work ; or in more recent times , because the job(s ) they have do n't pay the bills . The main difference is the experience of each class . The wealthy tend to be immune to even extreme economic failures : only drastic failures like the Great Depression cause them any problems . The working class , on the other hand , tend to be very depending on economic conditions . The Middle class can weather normal economic disruption , and only runs into serious problems in larger or longer economic disruption : the Dot - Bomb was a minor setback for the Middle Class , but the recent credit crisis / mortgage bubble / recession did hit the middle class . A smaller middle class means that more people are economically vulnerable ; meaning that smaller economic setbacks will be enough to cause large problems . This was n't an issue historically because there were n't as many poor : even people who were n't well off could at least take care of themselves .

Food is often preserved through a variety of methods to help it stay fresh for longer periods of time. One common method is by sealing the food in an airtight container or packaging. This helps to keep bacteria, fungi, and other microorganisms out, which can cause the food to spoil.

When you open a package of food, it is exposed to the air and the microorganisms in the environment, which can begin to break down the food and cause it to spoil. That's why it's important to store opened food in the refrigerator or freezer to help keep it fresh for as long as possible.

It's also important to keep in mind that while preserving methods can help extend the shelf life of food, they are not always foolproof. It's still important to pay attention to expiration dates and to follow safe food handling practices to ensure that the food you eat is safe and fresh.

When you take a picture of the TV screen, the camera is taking a still image of the screen at a specific moment in time. The camera is not able to process the information on the screen in the same way that your brain does, so the picture may look different than what you see with your own eyes. The horizontal lines that you see in the photo are called "scan lines." TVs display pictures by rapidly drawing each line of the picture on the screen, from top to bottom. Amputation is a surgical procedure that involves removing a limb or part of a limb, such as an arm or leg. The goal of the surgery is to remove damaged or diseased tissue, such as in the case of an infection or injury, or to help control chronic pain.

During the surgery, the doctor will first numb the area of the limb to be amputated, so the patient does not feel any pain. The doctor will then make an incision in the skin to access the limb and will use special medical tools, such as a saw or scissors, to carefully cut through the bone. The doctor may also use a device called a cautery pen to stop bleeding by sealing the blood vessels as they are cut.

After the limb has been removed, the doctor will close the incision with stitches or staples and may place a bandage or dressing over the wound. The patient will then be taken to a recovery room to rest and will be closely monitored for any complications.

It is important to note that amputation is a major surgery and is only done when other treatments, such as medications or physical therapy, are not effective in managing the condition. The decision to undergo amputation is not taken lightly and is made by a team of doctors and other healthcare professionals, in consultation with the patient and their family.

Everyone feels sad or down from time to time, and that's completely normal. However, when those feelings of sadness and hopelessness persist for a long time and interfere with a person's daily life, it might be a sign of depression, which is a mental illness. 
Depression is a medical condition that affects how a person thinks, feels, and behaves. It can cause a person to feel very sad and lose interest in things that they used to enjoy. It can also cause physical symptoms such as changes in appetite, difficulty sleeping, and low energy. 
If someone is experiencing these symptoms for a long time and they are causing problems in their daily life, such as difficulty going to work or school, not being able to take care of themselves, or having trouble maintaining relationships, it might be time to talk to a doctor or mental health professional. They can help determine if the person is experiencing depression and offer treatment options to help them feel better.

When you save a file on your computer, it gets stored on your hard drive (or "disk") in a series of blocks. These blocks are like a bunch of little boxes that the file gets divided into and stored in.

Sometimes, when you delete a file or move it to a different place on your hard drive, it leaves behind empty blocks where the file used to be. If you save a new file, it might get divided into blocks and stored in those empty blocks.

This is what's called "fragmentation." It's like a puzzle where the pieces are scattered all over the place instead of being next to each other. When the blocks of a file are scattered all over the hard drive, it takes the computer longer to find and open the file because it has to search for all the different blocks.

"Defragmenting" is the process of organizing the blocks on the hard drive so that the files are stored in as few blocks as possible and in a way that makes it easy for the computer to find them. Defragmenting can make your computer run faster because it has to do less work to find and open files.

To defragment your hard drive, you can use a built-in tool on your computer or a special program. The process can take a while, depending on how big your hard drive is and how many files you have. But once it's finished, your computer should be able to find and open files more quickly.

Examining the burn patterns: Arson fires often have distinctive burn patterns that are different from fires caused by other factors, such as electrical malfunctions. Examining physical evidence: Authorities may look for physical evidence, such as accelerants (substances that can help a fire spread), that may indicate that the fire was started deliberately. 4. Using specialized equipment: Authorities may use specialized equipment, such as thermal imaging cameras or gas chromatographs, to help them determine the cause of the fire. 5. Consulting with experts: Authorities may consult with experts, such as fire investigators or forensic scientists, to help them determine the cause of the fire. It's important to note that determining the cause of a fire can be complex and may require a thorough investigation. If you have any information about a fire, it's important to report it to the authorities so they can properly investigate.

The point at the very middle of the cone is the present -- that 's the moment you 're standing in . And the up and down axis is time . Up is forward , into the future . And down is backward , into the past . The pointiness of the cone represents the speed of light . And so the width of the cone at any given height ( and remember , height is time ) is the distance that light could have traveled from the place where an event happened since it happened . As more time passes , the cone gets wider because light could have traveled farther . Now , pretend you 're standing in that up and down axis , and at the very center is the time at which your ice cream fell on the ground . Nothing in the universe moves faster than light , but plenty of things move slower . So if something happened in the bottom half of the diagram OUTSIDE the cone , it would have to move faster than light in order to reach you before your ice cream fell , so it COULDN'T have POSSIBLY knocked your ice cream on the ground . Everything that could is in the circle that gets wider as you go farther back in time .

They are n't exactly trying to recreate the Big Bang conditions . They are trying to prove or disprove the theories that have been built up over the last 100 years or so about what matter and energy are and how they relate to each other . What happens when you cut up something into smaller and smaller pieces , like a grain of salt ? You eventually get molecules , and then atoms , and then things like protons , neutrons , and electrons . But you could n't really cut those things up with a knife , because no knife can possibly be sharp enough , and you would n't be able to see what you were doing anyway . Instead , physicists have learned to bang particles together at very high velocities and measure the bits that fly apart to see if they can make sense of what is happening , thus confirming or disproving their theories about what should happen . It turns out that you can learn more by banging them together with ever higher velocities , and there are things that you can virtually never observe at lower velocities . This is because higher velocities of the particles equates to higher energy available , and some subatomic interactions require an awful lot of energy . Of course , at the time of the Big Bang , there was a lot of energy concentrated in a small space , so we say that the conditions in the LHC are more like the BB than previous accelerators , because the LHC makes particles bang into each other with higher velocities than has * ever * been done before . There are several different theories for how all particles / forces come together and interact with each other , and each theory has some problems . The LHC is an attempt to figure out which theory is more right than the others , and get data that will help improve it . Or , even better , it will come up with results that * * do n't fit any * * of the existing theories . Then physicists will have to come up with new , better theories , and maybe one of those wo n't have so many problems .

Most people will accuse Microsoft of a litany of offenses : resting on their laurels , attempting to dominate and retard the Internet , security woes . Those are deliberate misunderstandings . The truth is a lot simpler , but also a lot more nuanced : Internet Explorer is bundled with Windows . Other browsers are not . The fact that IE is bundled with Windows has a number of implications , many of which have made and continue to make IE less attractive to the " savvy " internet user than other browsers . Anti - trust trial notwithstanding , IE is still considered a component of Windows within Microsoft . Its development is tied directly to Windows ; new releases of the browser closely follow new releases of Windows . Consider the following table : IE Version|Windows version ----:|:---- 1.0|Windows 95 1.5|Windows NT 4 2.0|Windows 95 OSR 1 3.0|Windows 95 OSR 2 4.0|Windows 98 5.0|Windows 98se 5.5|Windows ME 6.0|Windows XP 7.0|Windows XP SP2 8.0|Windows Vista 9.0|Windows 7 10.0|Windows 8 Because IE is tied to the operating system that powers 90+% of the world 's PCs , Microsoft is forced to be a lot more conservative and careful about the features it introduces to IE and the way it is developed . Every move it makes has huge repercussions on the Internet - at - large , for good or ill , whether Microsoft wants it to or not . They know this , and they take their responsibility ( and their power ) very seriously . And everyone else knows it as well . For many , their hatred of IE stems from their envy of what it represents : the power Microsoft has over personal computing . Take , for example , the huge issues that occurred with IE6 and Windows XP in the early 00s . Prior to that point , Microsoft 's sole focus with Windows and IE was to introduce as many new features as quickly as possible . They were competing fiercely with Netscape on the browser side , because they considered Netscape to be the gravest threat to their business . The Internet was just starting to truly become mainstream , but even then no - one really knew what that meant . Everyone was transitioning from Windows 98 , a DOS - based consumer OS to Windows XP which , being based on Windows NT , at least had some intrinsic security features . But Windows XP also had IE , a browser developed originally for Windows 95 , a browser with crazy shit like ActiveX , embedded fonts , a wonky but functional version of JavaScript and so on . Massive security vulnerabilities . And Windows itself had not been built to expect the kinds of threats the Internet would pose , simply because Microsoft did n't know what the threats * were * ; they had n't been conceived yet . Microsoft took a massive PR beating in the early 00s , as vulnerability after vulnerability was ruthlessly exploited in Windows XP and IE 6 , the latter having reached the height of its popularity after its only competition -- Netscape -- basically gave up . So Microsoft retreated . Instead of releasing the next version of Windows ( then codenamed Longhorn ) and with it , the next version of IE , they went to work adding the security features that Windows desperately needed . This delayed the release of Longhorn -- another huge PR problem for Microsoft -- but more importantly , it also delayed the release of IE 7 . The internet - at - large felt that delay . IE6 became entrenched . People got used to it . It had no real competition ; Netscape gave up , Firefox was too immature ( but was popular among the cool kids ) , Safari only ran on Macs and Chrome did n't exist yet . Technologies and standards for websites continued to advance ; CSS 2 got fleshed out , HTML 4 , XHTML 1 , new features in JavaScript . But IE 6 , frozen as it was , did n't support them . It became an anchor , holding everything back , its sheer popularity causing the Web to stagnate . Half a decade later , Web developers and savvy web users still have n't forgiven Microsoft for that . As a web developer , I count myself among them . It remains to be seen how long it will take before we forgive . Maybe we never will . I do n't , but at least I understand . Microsoft is trying mightily to win us back , of course . It largely completed the security retrofit with Windows Vista , which allowed it to focus on adding features again . IE9 represented a massive catch - up effort for IE , bringing it up to support the more stable chunks of HTML 5 and CSS 3 . But the ground it lost in the 00s allowed two new competitors -- Firefox and Chrome -- to step in and beat it to the punch . And they are n't as conservative as Microsoft has to be ; they can rapidly iterate their browsers , introducing new , whiz - bang features at a rapid pace , winning fans and users as they go . While Microsoft is forced to tread slowly and steadily , measuring every move they make , testing every feature they add , knowing that they ca n't afford any more major blunders . Such an attitude is prudent and wise , but it does n't win them fans . Look at the current kerfuffle with WebGL . Firefox and Chrome support it , which is why you can play Angry Birds inside the Chrome browser without downloading a single plugin . Microsoft wo n't add support for it to IE until it can reasonably guarantee it can do it without introducing massive security vulnerabilities , and The Internet hates them for it . And so it goes .

Sure! A silencer, also known as a suppressor, is a device that is attached to the barrel of a gun. Its purpose is to reduce the amount of noise and flash that is produced when the gun is fired. When a gun is fired, the bullet travels down the barrel and is propelled out of the gun by a small explosion that occurs in the chamber. This explosion creates a loud noise and a bright flash of light. A silencer works by using a series of baffles and other devices to slow down and cool the hot gases that are produced by the explosion. This helps to reduce the noise and flash that is produced when the gun is fired. Silencers do not completely eliminate the noise and flash from a gun, but they can make a significant difference. The amount of noise reduction will depend on the type of gun, the caliber of the bullet, and the design of the silencer. In general, a silencer can reduce the noise of a gun by 20-40 decibels, which is a significant reduction. I hope this helps to explain how silencers work! When you go to a higher altitude, the air pressure around you decreases. The pressure inside your ears is usually the same as the pressure outside your ears, so when you go to a higher altitude, the pressure inside your ears is greater than the pressure outside your ears. This can cause your ears to feel "plugged" or "full." 
To equalize the pressure, your body has a system that allows air to flow in and out of your middle ear, which is the space behind your eardrum. There's a small tube called the Eustachian tube that connects your middle ear to the back of your throat. When you swallow, yawn, or blow your nose, the muscles in the walls of the Eustachian tube open and close, allowing air to flow in and out of the middle ear and equalize the pressure. 
When you're at a higher altitude and the pressure inside your ears is greater than the pressure outside, your body will try to equalize the pressure by opening the Eustachian tube. When the tube opens, you may feel a "popping" sensation in your ears as the air flows in and the pressure equalizes. This is completely normal and nothing to be concerned about. It's just your body's way of adapting to the change in altitude.

The Tenth Amendment is still going strong . The states have what we call general police powers , which allow them to pass most any law they want ( so long as it 's constitutional ) . We just do n't ever think of a state passing a law as " exercising its Tenth Amendment rights . " The Constitutional clauses granting the federal government power have also generally been interpreted more and more broadly throughout American history , which reserves less power for the states , but they still have quite a bit . It 's also worth noting that the 10th amendment is basically superfluous . The Constitution already limits the federal government to the powers enumerated in the Constitution elsewhere . For this reason the only time the Supreme Court ever really mentions the 10th amendment is when they strike down a federal law that tries to force a state to enforce a federal regulation . For example , in 1998 the Court in Printz v. United States struck down a law requiring state law enforcement officials to conduct background checks on people purchasing handguns . The Court held that the federal government could n't coerce state officials into enforcing federal laws . However , the Tenth Amendment was mentioned along with other Constitutional provisions for generally standing for the principal of federalism - there 's nothing explicit in it that says " the federal government can not use the state government 's executive officers to enforce laws . "

Stand-up comedians are not immune from being sued for defamation of character or other legal actions. However, they are generally protected by the First Amendment of the United States Constitution, which guarantees the right to freedom of speech. This means that they are allowed to express their opinions and say things that might be critical or satirical, even if those things might be seen as offensive or inappropriate by some people. One reason why stand-up comedians are not sued as often is that they are generally seen as entertainers and their jokes are not taken too seriously. People typically understand that stand-up comedy is meant to be humorous and that the jokes are not meant to be taken literally. As a result, it is less likely that someone would consider bringing a legal action against a comedian for what they say in their stand-up routine. Additionally, there are certain legal defenses that can protect comedians from being sued for defamation of character or other legal actions. For example, if a comedian's jokes are based on a matter of public concern (such as politics or current events), they may be able to argue that their speech is protected by the First Amendment. Similarly, if a comedian's jokes are clearly exaggerated or humorous in nature, they may be able to argue that they were not intended to be taken seriously and therefore cannot be considered defamation. It's important to note that while stand-up comedians are generally protected by the First Amendment, they can still be sued for other types of actions, such as breach of contract or copyright infringement. Life insurance is a type of insurance that pays a benefit to your designated beneficiaries (such as your spouse or children) in the event of your death. It can help provide financial security and peace of mind to your loved ones in the event that you are no longer able to provide for them. Savings accounts are a type of financial product that allow you to save and earn interest on your money. While savings accounts can be a good way to set aside money for the future, they do not provide the same level of financial protection as life insurance. There are two main types of life insurance: term life insurance and permanent life insurance. Term life insurance is a type of insurance that provides coverage for a specific period of time, such as 10, 20, or 30 years. If you die during the term of the policy, your beneficiaries will receive the death benefit. If you outlive the term of the policy, the coverage will expire and you will not receive any benefit. Term life insurance is generally less expensive than permanent life insurance, but it does not build cash value over time. Permanent life insurance is a type of insurance that provides coverage for your entire life, as long as you continue to pay the premiums. Permanent life insurance policies also have a savings component, which allows you to build cash value over time. This cash value can be used to pay premiums or can be withdrawn or borrowed against. Permanent life insurance is generally more expensive than term life insurance, but it provides lifelong coverage and the ability to build cash value. During the Cold War, the city of Berlin was located deep within the territory of the German Democratic Republic (GDR), also known as East Germany. The city was divided into two parts: West Berlin, which was controlled by the Western powers (the United States, the United Kingdom, and France), and East Berlin, which was controlled by the Soviet Union. 
West Berlin was located on the western side of the Berlin Wall, which was a heavily fortified barrier that separated the two parts of the city. The wall was built in 1961 to prevent people from East Germany from fleeing to the West. It was guarded by East German soldiers, and anyone who tried to cross the wall illegally could be shot. 
Despite being surrounded by East Germany, West Berlin was not controlled by the GDR or the Soviet Union. Instead, it was a free and democratic city, similar to other cities in Western Europe. The Western powers provided the city with food, fuel, and other supplies to help it survive. They also allowed people from West Berlin to travel freely to other parts of Western Europe. 
East Berlin, on the other hand, was a communist city and was controlled by the GDR and the Soviet Union. It was not as open or free as West Berlin, and people who lived there had fewer freedoms and rights. 
The situation in Berlin remained like this until the end of the Cold War in the 1990s, when the Berlin Wall was finally taken down and the city was reunited.

In 1950 , Alan Turing wrote his landmark paper [ * COMPUTING MACHINERY AND INTELLIGENCE * ] ( URL_0 ) , where he probes the question " Can machines think ? " At the time , it was widely believed that there was something special about human intelligence and that computers would never be able to perform certain tasks , like understand language or play chess at a high level . Part of the paper was a point by point takedown of all the common objections to machine intelligence . As a part of this exercise , he proposed a thought experiment . If a human was an allowed to ask and unknown and unseen subject an unlimited number of questions , and could not determine whether it was a human or a computer , then we might consider the possibility the computer might have some kind of intelligence . This has come to be known as the Turing Test . The Turing Test has been * * extremely misunderstood * * . Many people take it to be a definitive test of real computer intelligence , but Turing never meant it this way . Instead it was more of a thought provoking notion to get people to consider exactly what machine intelligence meant . There are modern Turing Test competitions , but in general , they are less about machine intelligence , and more about human psychology , and how humans might be deceived by clever programming tricks .

"Invaluable" and "valuable" are synonyms because they both describe something that is worth a lot or has a lot of worth or importance. They can be used interchangeably in many contexts.

On the other hand, "destructible" and "indestructible" are not synonyms because they have opposite meanings. "Destructible" means something that can be destroyed or broken, while "indestructible" means something that cannot be destroyed or broken. They cannot be used interchangeably.

Here's an example to help you understand:

Imagine you have a very special and rare vase that you value a lot. You would say that the vase is valuable or invaluable because it has a lot of worth to you.

Printing more money can't fix problems because it doesn't necessarily create more wealth or resources. When a government or central bank prints more money, it increases the supply of money in the economy. However, if the amount of goods and services produced in the economy does not increase at the same rate, then the value of each unit of currency, like a dollar, will go down. This is because there will be more dollars chasing the same amount of goods and services, which means that each dollar will be worth less because it can buy fewer goods and services. Think about it this way: imagine that there is a pie that represents all the goods and services produced in an economy. If there are more dollars in circulation but the size of the pie stays the same, then each person will only get a smaller slice of the pie, even if they have more dollars. On the other hand, if the size of the pie increases (i.e., if there is more production of goods and services), then each person can have a bigger slice of the pie, even if the number of dollars stays the same. It's also important to note that printing more money can lead to inflation, which is when the overall price level in an economy increases over time. This can be caused by an increase in the money supply, among other things. When prices go up, it means that the same amount of money will buy fewer goods and services, which can be a problem for people and businesses that rely on a fixed income or have fixed prices for their goods and services. Ok firstly , head over to /r / homebrewing to see it in action ! Secondly , here 's a quick run through of the general process : 1 . The Wort is produced by mixing a starch source ( most often malted and crushed grains / barley a.k.a malts ) with hot water , this step is known as Mashing . This process takes a few hours , and causes the starches to break down into sugars . 2 . The Wort is drained from the malts , and the malts rinsed to remove as much of the sweet wort as possible . The sweet Wort and associated washings are trasnfered into a kettle ( known as a copper ) , and boiled for a few hours . This boils off some of the water , and produces a more concentrated Wort , allowing for a more efficient fermentation later on . The boiling also kills off any remaining enzymes / bacteria in the Wort , effectively sterylizing it . During the boil , hops are pitched into the Wort to add a bitter flavour . The longer the hops are boiled , the more bitter the final brew will be , but the less light - hoppy aroma you get on the palate . Careful selection of hop variety , as well as knowing when to pitch the hops is essential in producing the champion brews ! 3 . After the Wort is boiled , it 's cooled ready for the yeast . If the wort stays too hot it 'll kill the yeast , and you wo n't get any beer . Some breweries may hop the wort at this late stage , to give a powerfully hoppy aroma to the final brew . The hopped and cooled Wort is transfered to the fermenter , where the yeast is then pitched . During fermentation , the yeast digests the sugars in the Wort , producing ethanol ( alcohol - gets you drunk ) and carbon dioxide ( makes it gassy / fizzy ) . The fermentation step can take anywhere from days to months , depending on the brew .. but this long period allows any particulate matter to settle out to the bottom of the fermenter , resulting in a clear beer . 4 . Once the fermentation has finished , the final beer product is bottled / canned / casked . Then either stored on racks to mature , or sold and enjoyed straight away ! The different types of beers are usually achieved by the types of grains / hops used , as well as the stage in which the hops are pitched , how many hoppings are carried out etc .. sometimes the beer undergoes a few fermentations too .. With beer being up to 11,000 + years old , we 've had a long long time to perfect our methods and develop lots of different types !

Card counting is a technique used by some blackjack players to try and determine whether the next hand is likely to be favorable to the player or to the dealer. It is based on the idea that, in a deck of cards, there are more face cards (jacks, queens, and kings) than small cards (2 through 6). Since face cards are worth 10 points and small cards are worth less, a player who knows that there are more small cards left in the deck may be more likely to win the hand.

Now, casinos don't like players to use card counting because it gives the player an advantage over the house. So, they have a few ways to try and catch people who are doing it. One way is to watch how the player bets. If the player suddenly starts betting a lot of money when the deck is favorable, that might be a sign that the player is counting cards. Casinos might also use surveillance cameras to watch players and see if they are doing anything unusual, like keeping track of the cards that have been played. Finally, some casinos will ban players who are caught counting cards, or they might ask them to leave and not come back.

The way we measure time has evolved over the course of history. The base 60 system that we use for measuring time today (with 60 seconds in a minute and 60 minutes in an hour) dates back to the ancient Sumerians, who lived in what is now modern-day Iraq.

One of the main reasons the Sumerians used a base 60 system is because it is a highly composite number, which means it can be evenly divided by a large number of other integers. For example, 60 can be evenly divided by 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 10, 12, 15, 20, and 30. This made it easier for the Sumerians to divide time into smaller increments, which was helpful for their calendars and other time-keeping activities.

Using a base 10 system (like we do for counting) would not have the same convenient properties for dividing time into smaller increments. For example, if we had 100 seconds in a minute and 100 minutes in an hour, we would have to use factors of 100 to divide time into smaller increments, which would not be as convenient as using factors of 60.

So, to sum it up: we use a base 60 system for measuring time because it has convenient properties for dividing time into smaller increments, and this system has been used for many centuries.

First , voter fraud is already extremely rare . Here 's why . Only people who are registered to vote are allowed to vote . When you register , you have to provide a social security number , driver 's license number , or some other proof of citizenship . There are several checks and balances made to make it difficult to fraudulently register . When you vote , you have to provide the name of someone who 's registered to vote . They check that every person only votes once . In rare cases where the same person tries to vote twice , or someone tries to vote in place of someone else , this is easily caught and they investigate and throw out the invalid ballots . Between 2000 and 2010 [ there were only 13 cases of in - person voter fraud ] ( URL_0 ) , where someone either tried to vote twice or claimed they were someone else . Only 13 ! And all of them were caught , no fraudulent votes were ever counted . So the first part of the answer is , voter fraud is so rare that we do n't need new measures to prevent it ! Here 's the other part of the answer : Approximately 10 % of U.S. Citizens do not have a photo ID . Many do n't need it or do n't want it . Photo IDs usually cost money , but voting is free . So requiring photo ID does n't prevent any fraud , but it does either exclude some people from voting , or adds a financial burden for them to get the photo ID they otherwise do n't need . Why do Democrats oppose it more ? Because most of the people who do n't have an ID vote Democratic . If the people who do n't have an ID voted Republican , you 'd probably see some Democrats try to add voter ID laws and Republicans oppose it - but that would n't make it right . An unbiased analysis still shows that requiring photo ID is not * necessary * .

You can get an approximation ( estimate , if you prefer ) of the golden ratio from lucas / fibonacci numbers by taking one element from the sequence , then dividing it by the previous element . So , for fibonacci numbers ( 1 , 1 , 2 , 3 , 5 , 8 , 13 , 21 , ... ) , your approximations are 1/1 , 2/1 , 3/2 , 5/3 , 8/5 , 13/8 , ... and for Lucas numbers ( 2 , 1 , 3 , 4 , 7 , 11 , 18 , ... ) they 're 1/2 , 3/1 , 4/3 , 7/4 , 11/7 , 18/11 , ... Because both of these are integer sequences , and the golden ratio is irrational , you can never get the exact value . However , the further along you are in either sequence , the better the approximation . So ... how good are these approximations ? That 's where the Lucas numbers are " superior " to the fibonacci sequence : After a frankly wonky start ( because lucas numbers start weird , with the first term being bigger than the second ) , if you take the same number of steps ( that is , for both sequences dividing the 11th element by the 10th element , or dividing the 28th element by the 27th element , or whatever else ) , then doing that same amount of work using the Lucas numbers gets you an approximation that 's closer to the true value than you 'd get with Fibonacci numbers .

Gaslighting is a form of emotional abuse in which someone manipulates another person into doubting their own memories, perceptions, or judgment. It can be a very subtle and insidious form of abuse, and it can be difficult to recognize if it's happening to you. Imagine that you and your partner are having a conversation and they say something that you know is not true. When you try to point out the mistake, they deny ever saying it or try to convince you that you are mistaken. They might even go so far as to present evidence or provide an explanation that contradicts your memory of the event. Over time, this can cause you to doubt your own perception of reality and feel confused or uncertain about what is true and what is not. Gaslighting can be particularly harmful because it erodes the trust and emotional security that are fundamental to a healthy relationship. It can also lead to feelings of anxiety, self-doubt, and depression. It's important to be aware of the signs of gaslighting and to seek help if you think you are being subjected to this form of abuse.

When you lease a new vehicle, you are essentially renting it from the dealership or the manufacturer for a set period of time, usually two to four years. You will make monthly payments for the duration of the lease, but at the end of the lease period, you will need to return the vehicle to the dealership. Leasing can be a good option if you don't want to commit to owning a car for a long time, you want to drive a new car every few years, or you don't want to worry about the maintenance and repair costs of an older car.

On the other hand, financing a new vehicle means taking out a loan to pay for the car, and then making monthly payments to pay off the loan over a set period of time, usually three to seven years. Once you have paid off the loan, you own the car. Financing can be a good option if you want to own the car and keep it for a long time, you want to customize the car or make changes to it, or you don't want to have to worry about returning the car at the end of the lease period.

Ultimately, the decision to lease or finance a new vehicle will depend on your personal circumstances, financial situation, and long-term goals. If you are not sure which option is best for you, it can be helpful to talk to a financial advisor or a trusted mechanic to get more information and advice.

Two ways . The United States currently spends more money than it is able to raise by taxation -- this is called the deficit . To make up the difference , the Treasury raises money by selling securities . A security is basically a financial instrument where you give the government a certain amount of money , and the government gives you a certain ( larger ) amount of money at a later date . Some of these securities mature ( come due ) very quickly , while others do not mature for 10 - 30 years . So selling these things is how the US raises money it can not raise through taxation . When the debt ceiling is reached , the Treasury is no longer authorized to sell bonds . We will default on the debt we owe to parties who own securities that are coming due , because the Treasury will not be authorized to raise more money to meet those obligations . As to who owns the bulk of US treasury securities , a little over 1/3 of the debt is owed to overseas investors . The rest is owed to average everyday Americans who invested in securities , larger institutional investors like pensions , and so on . The single biggest holder of Treasury securities is actually the Social Security trust fund .
